title: Web Dev Lesson 1
content:
Full technical lesson content for Web Dev 1. The Document Object Model (DOM) is a programming interface for web documents. It represents the page so that programs can change the document structure, style, and content. The DOM represents the document as nodes and objects; that way, programming languages can connect to the page. A Web page is a document that can be either displayed in the browser window or as the HTML source. In both cases, it is the same document but the Document Object Model (DOM) representation allows it to be manipulated. As an object-oriented representation of the web page, it can be modified with a scripting language such as JavaScript. The W3C DOM and WHATWG DOM standards are implemented in most modern browsers. Many browsers extend the standard, so care must be exercised when using them on the web where documents may be accessed by various browsers with different DOMs. For example, the standard DOM specifies that the getElementsByTagName method in the code below must return a list of all the

elements in the document. This is a very powerful way to access and manipulate elements on a page without knowing their IDs or classes beforehand. Understanding the DOM tree structure—where every element is a node—is fundamental to modern web development. Parent nodes, child nodes, and sibling nodes form the architecture of every website you visit. By using methods like appendChild, removeChild, and replaceChild, developers can dynamically update the user interface without requiring a full page reload, leading to the smooth experiences we expect from modern web applications today.

quiz.question: What does DOM stand for?
Which language is primarily used to manipulate the DOM?
Is the DOM a programming language?
